notes: Main Atropos paper; open-access PMC copy can be archived locally.
Main Cutadapt paper; archive the open-access article together with the maintained upstream repository and docs.
Main fastp methods paper; use the publisher DOI landing page as the citation locator and keep the open-access PMC/PubMed records as access/index locators.

FastQValidator does not currently expose a clear canonical paper in the governed upstream materials; use the maintained repository as the durable software-citation root. The VirIdAl pipeline paper cites the fastQValidator GitHub repository as software evidence, not as a dedicated methods paper.
FastQC does not currently have a clear canonical paper in the upstream materials; use the project homepage and upstream repository as the durable software-citation root.
Main UMI-tools paper; archive the open-access publisher article together with the upstream repository and docs.
SeqKit stats is part of the SeqKit project and uses the same primary publication as the seqkit tool surface.
Main SeqKit paper; open-access PMC copy can be archived locally.
Main SeqFu paper; open-access PMC copy can be archived locally.
Main DIAMOND methods paper; use the Nature DOI landing page as the citation locator and PubMed as the index locator.
Main DADA2 methods paper; use the Nature article when licensed access is available.
Main FastUniq paper; open-access PMC copy can be archived locally.
Main SeqPurge paper; keep it paired with the ngs-bits source tree used for the implementation.
Main Morgulis et al. SDUST paper cited by the NCBI DustMasker source page; use the DOI publisher landing page as the citation locator and keep PubMed/source-page locators as supporting evidence.
Main VSEARCH paper; archive the open-access PMC copy and keep it tied to the upstream repository packet.
Main AdapterRemoval v2 paper; archive the open-access PMC copy and keep it tied to the source repository packet.
Main AlienTrimmer paper; ScienceDirect publisher PDF is archived locally. Keep the 2014 Frontiers commentary as supporting performance context, not as a replacement for the 2013 methods paper.
Supporting Frontiers commentary on AlienTrimmer sensitivity in short-insert paired-end reads; archive the open-access PMC/Frontiers PDF as context for trimming claims.
Main BayesHammer paper; archive the open-access Springer/BMC PDF and keep it tied to the SPAdes source tree.
Internal planned FASTQ contract-harness stages resolve through the governed bijux-genomics repository archive; do not treat this as a standalone peer-reviewed tool paper.
BBDuk currently resolves through the official BBTools documentation and release surface rather than a clearly governed standalone paper.
Main BBMerge paper; archive the open-access publisher or PMC copy and keep it tied to the BBTools packet.
Main Bowtie 2 paper; use the Nature DOI landing page as citation locator and keep PMC/source repository locators as access and implementation evidence.
bowtie2-build is the governed index-building surface for Bowtie 2; archive the same Langmead and Salzberg paper context under a build-surface root so closure checks stay tool-specific.
Main Centrifuge paper; use the Genome Research DOI landing page as citation locator and keep PMC/source repository locators as access and implementation evidence.
Clumpify currently resolves through the official BBTools documentation and release surface rather than a clearly governed standalone paper.
fastq-scan currently resolves through the maintained source repository and project page rather than a canonical methods paper.
Main FastQ Screen paper; archive the open-access F1000Research/PMC PDF and keep it tied to the source repository and Babraham project page.

FASTX-Toolkit currently resolves through source/project-homepage evidence rather than a clearly governed canonical paper; fastx_clipper is treated as a FASTX-Toolkit component rather than a standalone publication.
Main FLASH paper used for FLASH2 lineage evidence; archive the PMC/Europe PMC PDF and keep the publisher locator.
Main fqtools paper; archive the PMC/Europe PMC PDF and keep it tied to the source repository packet.
Main Kaiju paper; archive the open-access Nature Communications article/PDF and keep it tied to the source repository and project documentation packet.
Main Kraken 2 paper; archive the open-access Genome Biology article/PDF and keep it tied to the source repository and project documentation packet.
Main KrakenUniq paper; archive the open-access Genome Biology article/PDF and keep it tied to the source repository and project documentation packet.
Main leeHom paper; archive the PMC/Europe PMC PDF and keep it tied to the source repository packet.
Main Lighter paper; archive the open-access PMC copy and keep it tied to the source repository packet.
Main MultiQC paper; archive the PMC/Europe PMC PDF and keep it tied to the source repository packet.
Main Musket paper; open-access OUP PDF is archived locally and kept tied to the release packet.
Main PEAR paper; archive the PMC/Europe PMC PDF and keep it tied to the release packet.
Main PRINSEQ paper used for the governed PRINSEQ++ implementation; archive the PMC/Europe PMC PDF and keep the publisher locator.
Main Rcorrector paper; archive the open-access publisher article/PDF and keep it tied to the source repository packet.

Seqtk currently resolves through its maintained source repository rather than a clearly governed canonical methods paper. The FastQDesign paper cites software repositories directly, including repository-style software evidence rather than a seqtk methods paper.
Main Skewer paper; archive the open-access publisher article/PDF and keep it tied to the source repository packet.
Main SortMeRNA paper; archive the open-access Bioinformatics article/PDF and keep it tied to the source repository packet.

Main STAR paper; use the Bioinformatics DOI landing page as citation locator and keep PMC/source repository locators as access and implementation evidence. The PMC OA utility reports this PMCID is not Open Access, so a local PDF payload still requires access-compliant acquisition.

Trim Galore has no dedicated peer-reviewed methods paper; use the Zenodo software-release DOI as the strongest citation locator, keep the repository/project page for software provenance, and retain the Nature Communications usage paper only as supporting usage context.
Main Trimmomatic paper; archive the open-access PMC copy and keep it tied to the upstream release page.
